cases.title: Member browses, opens, and edits a record
cases.goal: A tenant member finds an existing record in their tenant, opens its detail view, and updates a field.
preconditions: Member is authenticated against the tenant (post-/_reference/dev-login or production auth flow).
Tenant context is selected (`tenant` route already visited or session-pinned).
triggers: Member clicks 'Records' in the shell nav.

mainFlow.action: Member navigates to the records list page.
Member clicks a record row to open its detail view.
Member clicks 'Edit'; the form route loads.
Member edits a field and submits.
App returns to the detail view showing the update.

postconditions: The record reflects the edited value.
An audit event is recorded for the PATCH (per stynx auditing convention).
acceptanceCriteria: PATCH /records/:id returns 200 with the updated record body.
The next GET /records/:id reflects the new field value.
Read endpoints meet stynx SLO: p50 latency < 200ms, p95 < 800ms under nominal load.

cases.title: Member creates a new record
cases.goal: A tenant member creates a brand-new record in their tenant.
preconditions: Member is authenticated against the tenant.
triggers: Member clicks 'New record' from the records list.

mainFlow.action: Member navigates to the new-record form.
Member fills the form and submits.
App navigates to the detail view of the new record.

postconditions: The new record exists with the supplied values.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /records returns 201 with the new resource id.

cases.title: Member soft-deletes a record and restores it from trash
cases.goal: A tenant member removes a record (soft-delete) and later restores it via the trash view.

mainFlow.action: Member opens the record detail view.
Member clicks 'Delete'; the API soft-deletes the record.
Member clicks 'Restore' on the soft-deleted record.

alternateFlows.when: Tenant-admin chooses hard-delete instead of restore.

postconditions: After restore: the record is queryable again via GET /records/:id.
acceptanceCriteria: After DELETE /records/:id, GET /records/:id returns 404 (or 410) for the soft-deleted resource.
After POST /records/:id/restore, GET /records/:id returns the resource with no `deleted_at` set.

cases.title: Reference dev-login flow
cases.goal: An anonymous user opens the reference app, picks a demo tenant, and obtains a session token via the dev-login endpoint (reference-application convenience; production deployments would use Cognito JWT).
preconditions: The /_reference/dev-login endpoint is reachable (gated by the reference-app deployment, NOT production).
triggers: User visits the app root, sees the login page.

mainFlow.action: App routes anonymous user to the login page. Unknown paths fall through the catch-all (path: '**') redirect to root, which the auth-guard funnels back here.
User selects a demo tenant; app sends a dev-login.
App stores the session token; user becomes a tenant-member.
App verifies the issued token round-trips.
App navigates to the tenant-selection or dashboard route.

postconditions: User has an active session in the chosen demo tenant.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /_reference/dev-login returns 201 with a Bearer-shaped token.
GET /_reference/auth-verify returns 200 against the issued token.
